title: erodium praecox
intro: Erodium praecox, also known as the common storksbill or early erodium, is a species of flowering plant in the geranium family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. Erodium praecox has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, as well as to aid in the healing of wounds and ulcers. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
